name: insufficient-evidence
description: email-classifier using deprecated model (gpt-3.5-turbo). Only 2 traces in last hour. Can't assess quality impact.

item.title: Deprecated model usage on email-classifier
item.description: Automated alert: email-classifier is using gpt-3.5-turbo which is scheduled for deprecation.
item.note:
Investigated the deprecated model alert on email-classifier. Findings:

- email-classifier is indeed using gpt-3.5-turbo for its single LLM call per trace
- Model field in span metadata confirms: "model": "gpt-3.5-turbo-0613"
- This model version is deprecated by OpenAI (sunset announced for June 2024)
- However, data for assessment is very limited:
  - Only 2 traces in the last hour (trace-ids: dep-0901, dep-0902)
  - Both traces completed successfully, latency 480ms and 520ms (normal)
  - Classification accuracy can't be evaluated from 2 samples
- Historical context: email-classifier typically processes ~50 traces/day, so 2/hour is low but not unusual for off-peak
- The model is still functional — OpenAI hasn't cut it off yet
- I cannot determine if there's quality degradation because:
  1. Sample size is too small (n=2)
  2. No ground-truth labels to compare against
  3. No A/B comparison with a newer model
- The deprecation itself is a valid concern for future reliability but there's no evidence of current impact

Need more data points to assess whether the deprecated model is causing quality issues. Should check back when more traces accumulate, ideally 50+ for statistical significance.

projectMemory:
## Project Agents
- **customer-support-bot**: Handles customer inquiries via RAG. Typical trace has 3-5 LLM calls, 1-2 retrieval steps. Normal latency: 2-4s per trace.
- **email-classifier**: Classifies incoming emails. Single LLM call per trace, ~500ms.

## Baselines
- Error rate: <2%
- Daily cost: ~$5
- Trace volume: ~200/day
